title: Tectaria neglecta
taxonomy: Tectaria neglecta is classified under the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. It is further classified as belonging to the genus Tectaria, which is a genus of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species T. neglecta is closely related to other species within the genus, such as T. crenatiloba and T. hercoglossa. The classification of T. neglecta is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its frond shape and leaf arrangement. This classification is used to identify and distinguish T. neglecta from other fern species.
uses: Tectaria neglecta is reportedly used in traditional medicine for various purposes. In some regions, the plant is used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Tectaria neglecta is also used in traditional remedies for skin conditions and wounds. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential benefits.
